title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Gwendolyn Reed provides a statement to Inspector Primer, claiming she saw a dead woman with golden hair and a purple face in a hillside villa hallway, whom she identifies as Helen. Gwendolyn suggests that someone framed her father for the murder of Lily Kimber, noting that Dr. Penrose and Dr. Kennedy believed her father was incapable of such an act. She also shares her speculations regarding the impersonated calls received by Walter Fane and J.J. Avery. Inspector Primer notes that Lily Kimber was murdered between 2:20 and 2:45 PM and emphasizes the need for verification over speculation.

In the garden, Inspector Primer meets Miss Marple, who compares the deep roots of bindweed to the long-standing nature of the murder case. A policeman then announces that a body has been found. Jules returns to the house pale to inform Gwendolyn of the discovery. Upon seeing the remains, Mrs. Coker suffers a severe physical reaction; despite Gwendolyn providing her with brandy, Mrs. Coker becomes unresponsive with terror and is taken to the hospital. A forensic pathologist notes that Mrs. Coker might have died without medical intervention. Inspector Primer questions Gwendolyn about her recent consumption of alcohol, and she explains that she and Jules had been drinking whiskey instead of brandy. After the police depart, Jervis eats lunch and joins the others, leaving Gwendolyn alone in the house.
